evidence.snippet: This system can lower an entire aircraft to the ground in the event of a loss of control, structural failure, or other in-flight emergency.

#### The only one offering the Certified FAA/EASA Part 23
Developed and certified FAA/EASA Part 23 systems for Cessna 172, Cessna 182, Bristell B23, Elixir, Tecnam P-Mentor and others coming.

items.notes: BRS Aerospace's entire product line is whole aircraft recovery parachute systems (WARPS), including for unmanned vehicles.

evidence.snippet: More than four decades of design and engineering experience working hand-in-hand with aircraft manufacturers to create customized and reliable safety solutions.

drone_supply_chain_role.notes: BRS primarily serves manned GA aircraft but a 2017 press release (resource-0008 L37) explicitly names 'unmanned vehicles' among served segments. Their recovery systems are installed on third-party platforms.

items.name: BRS System for Cessna 172/182
items.product_type: FAA/EASA Part 23 certified whole aircraft recovery parachute system (retrofit)

evidence.snippet: BRS is the only Whole Aircraft Recovery Parachute System certified as a retrofit for the Cessna 172s and Cessna 182s.

items.name: Cirrus Airframe Parachute System (CAPS)
items.product_type: whole aircraft recovery parachute system for Cirrus SR20/SR22

evidence.snippet:
#### Developed the Cirrus Airframe Parachute System
Collaborated with Cirrus Aircraft to develop the first Recovery Parachute System to be used on a line of type certified aircraft: the Cirrus SR20, followed by the Cirrus SR22 in 2001.

items.name: Whole Aircraft Recovery Parachute System (WARPS)
items.product_type: ballistic-rocket-deployed parachute recovery system for light sport, experimental, ultralight, and unmanned aircraft

evidence.snippet: #### Ballistic Recovery Systems was founded in 1980 by Boris Popov of Saint Paul, Minnesota, after he survived a 400-foot (120 m) fall in a partially collapsed hang glider in 1975. As a result, Popov invented the WARPS, the Whole Aircraft Recovery Parachute System.

tagline: Manufactures ballistic whole-aircraft recovery parachute systems for manned aviation (Cirrus SR20/22, Cessna 172/182, 450+ aircraft models). FAA/EASA Part 23 certified; claims 504 lives saved since 1980. No public evidence of NDAA compliance, Blue UAS listing, or unmanned-systems applications; market focus appears exclusively manned GA.
